message:
feat(view): integrate MessageRenderCache into conversation renderer (PERF-1)

Wires the MessageRenderCache (added in the previous PERF-1 commit)
into the actual conversation rendering pipeline. This completes the
render memoization optimization by:

1. Extracting `render_single_message(&self, msg)` from
   `build_conversation_content()`:
   - Moves per-message rendering logic (User, Assistant, Tool, System
     roles) into a dedicated method, enabling cached and uncached paths
     to share the same rendering code
   - The streaming response (current_response/current_thinking) remains
     outside the cache and always renders fresh

2. Integrating cache lookup/store in `build_conversation_content()`:
   - For each message, computes a `MessageCacheKey` and checks the
     cache before rendering
   - Cache hits skip rendering entirely, using the stored string
   - Cache misses render and store the result for subsequent frames

3. Adding cache invalidation calls at all state-change points:
   - `invalidate_all()` on thinking visibility toggle (Ctrl+T keybind
     and hideThinkingBlock config change) and tool expand/collapse
     toggle — these change rendering of ALL messages
   - `clear()` on conversation clear (/clear command), session switch
     (/session load), new session creation, tree UI branch switch,
     agent completion, and memory truncation — these replace the
     message list entirely

Files touched:
- src/interactive/view.rs: Core refactor (render_single_message +
  cache integration in build_conversation_content)
- src/interactive.rs: invalidate_all on thinking toggle, clear on
  memory truncation
- src/interactive/agent.rs: clear on agent completion
- src/interactive/commands.rs: clear on /clear, session load, new
  session
- src/interactive/keybindings.rs: invalidate_all on Ctrl+T and
  tool expand toggle
- src/interactive/tree_ui.rs: clear on tree branch switch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
